name: ASAP5
description: Optimized for detecting subthreshold activity, with the steepest F-V slope near resting potential.

spectrum.name: ASAP5
spectrum.note: Excitation digitized from Fig. 1a of the ASAP7y paper (Hao 2026). Emission is the shared cpGFP-family profile — ASAP5 emission is not reported in this figure.

kinetics.note: Bi-exponential fits at 37°C (Table S2). Activation: τ-fast 0.8 ms (95%), τ-slow 44.2 ms (5%) → amplitude-weighted mean 2.97 ms. Deactivation: τ-fast 1.1 ms (63%), τ-slow 5.2 ms (37%) → amplitude-weighted mean 2.62 ms. The paper headlines the fast components (0.78/1.12 ms).

Bi-exponential fits at 23°C (Table S2). Activation: τ-fast 2.6 ms (92%), τ-slow 45.3 ms (8%) → amplitude-weighted mean 6.02 ms. Deactivation: τ-fast 4.3 ms (75%), τ-slow 55.9 ms (25%) → amplitude-weighted mean 17.2 ms. The paper headlines the fast components (2.61/4.30 ms).

dynamicRangeData.note: Steady-state -ΔF/F for a -70 to +30 mV (100-mV) depolarizing step in HEK293A cells under one-photon widefield voltage clamp; -57 ± 2% at 23°C and -57 ± 3% at 37°C (Table S2). The F-V curve continues to a ~-60% plateau by +80 mV over the wider -70 to +80 mV sweep.
Response to a 100-mV depolarizing step from -70 mV at 33°C in HEK293A cells under 2P resonant scanning at 940 nm (Ext. Fig. 1b, n = 7 cells). Reduced response relative to JEDI-2P (41.8%) under 2P.

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, visual cortex L2/3 pyramidal neurons, awake behaving, in vivo, soma-targeted ASAP5-Kv (ULoVE 2P)
apWidthData.note: Optical action-potential waveform width (full width at half maximum) of the rapid post-threshold optical spike, recorded by ULoVE random-access 2P microscopy (920 nm) at 7142.9 Hz (two patterns/cell) in layer-2/3 visual cortex pyramidal neurons of awake behaving mice. Main text: 'FWHM of the rapid optical spike varies from sub-millisecond to 2 ms.' Bar (mean) digitized from the Fig. S4E 'Spike FWHM' panel ≈1.3 ms; the 11 per-neuron dots span 0.69-2.15 ms (mean of dots 1.32 ms), n = 11. Mammalian in-vivo cortex (~37°C; exact recording temperature not stated numerically). Axis-tick calibration ±~0.05 ms.

apWidthData.sample: Rat, cultured cortical neurons, current-evoked APs, in vitro
apWidthData.note: Cross-GEVI entry from the ASAP7y paper (primary subject ASAP7y). Optical single-AP FWHM digitized from the averaged optical AP waveform in Fig. 1c (the cyan ΔF/F trace — average of n=7 cultured cortical neurons, plotted above the averaged electrical AP), calibrated to the panel's 20 ms scale bar and overlay-verified ≈ 6.8 ms (vs ASAP7y ≈ 10.3 ms in the same panel). This is a DIFFERENT regime from the Neuron-2024 ULoVE entry above (in-vivo 2P rapid post-threshold optical spike, 1.3 ms): here it is the slower full averaged optical AP waveform in culture. CAVEATS: the paper does not report this experiment's frame rate (samplingRate omitted), modality, or temperature for this panel; the ≈7 ms width is kinetics-dominated, not frame-rate-limited.

sensitivityData.note: Mean peak -ΔF/F per current-evoked action potential in cultured rat hippocampal neurons at room temperature, recorded by camera at 800 fps under one-photon widefield illumination (Fig. 1F, n = 10 cells). ~2-fold larger than ASAP3 (~17%).
Peak -ΔF/F per action potential in Drosophila TPN-II neurons in vivo under 940-nm two-photon excitation; ASAP7y responded 81% more strongly in the same prep

brightnessData.note: Per-neuron relative brightness in primary rat cortical neurons (Kv2.1-tagged constructs), normalized to ASAP4e-Kv = 1.0

photostabilityData.note: Effective irradiance ~20 mW/mm² (=46/2.3): Fig. S5E is an SNR-matched comparison vs Voltron2525-Kv — ASAP5-Kv was imaged at 46 mW/mm² but its time-axis was multiplied by 2.3 to plot the curve at the irradiance that yields the same SNR as Voltron2525-Kv at 46 mW/mm² (STAR Methods), so the plotted curve represents ~20 mW/mm². ~83% remaining at 1 min, digitized from the cyan ASAP5-Kv curve (full curve in photobleach[] below). CAVEAT: curves were normalized to the value at 0.5 s (the transient bright state in the first 0.5 s is excluded), so this slightly underestimates true bleaching; the cross-GEVI displayPhotostab normalization to 100 mW/mm² assumes linear photobleaching dose, which is consistent with the paper's own ×2.3 time-axis rescaling but should be read as approximate for this non-standard measurement.

photobleach.note: Digitized (cyan ASAP5-Kv curve) from Fig. S5E, an SNR-matched photostability comparison vs Voltron2525-Kv. Acquired at 46 mW/mm², but the time-axis was multiplied by 2.3 (STAR Methods) so the plotted curve represents ASAP5-Kv photobleaching at the SNR-matched effective irradiance of ~20 mW/mm² (=46/2.3) in real-time-equivalent seconds. Normalized to the 0.5 s value — the brief transient bright state in the first 0.5 s is excluded, so F0 is the post-transient baseline (true bleaching is slightly faster than shown). Decays to ~83% at 60 s; monoexponential fit τ≈301 s (r²=0.99); t75≈87 s is EXTRAPOLATED beyond the 60 s window. intensityMWmm2 intentionally omitted: this SNR-matched / time-rescaled / 0.5 s-normalized measurement is non-standard, so it stands alone and is NOT linearly dose-scaled to the 100 mW/mm² reference or compared across GEVIs.

Cross-GEVI entry from the ASAP7y paper (Hao 2026; primary subject ASAP7y). Digitized (blue ASAP5 curve) from Fig. 2g: normalized fluorescence over 1 min of continuous two-photon imaging of Mi1 neurons in vivo (Drosophila) on an AOD random-access microscope at 940 nm (~16 mW post-objective, STAR Methods 'sensor comparison in Mi1'), plotted against ASAP7y at 940 nm (green, plateaus near 1.0). ASAP5 decays to ~0.85 at 60 s and plateaus (offset-monoexponential τ≈11.6 s, plateau c≈0.86, r²≈0.98). t75 OMITTED: the curve never falls to 75% of F0 within the 60 s window (too photostable at this power) — honest lower bound is t75 > 60 s; not extrapolated. 2P power-only measurement: intensityMWmm2 intentionally omitted (no mW/mm² given), NOT dose-scaled to the 100 mW/mm² 1P reference and NOT compared across the 1P entry above; stands alone. Co-figure GEVI ASAP7y (940 nm) shares this source + Fig. 2g for cross-overlay.
